events_with_summaries.status: Review for Publish review - No Comms
events_with_summaries.summary_text: Affirm is no longer accepted for in-store payments; customers can still manage existing Affirm purchases and returns as usual. Walmart’s buy-now-pay-later option is now OnePay Later (Klarna), and stores should update signage and ensure teams know the change.

events_with_summaries.title: Join the DSD Office Hours for Real-Time Support
events_with_summaries.status: Review for Publish review - No Comms

Backroom: Join the DSD Office Hours for Real-Time Support

Join our bi-weekly DSD Office Hours on Tuesday, March third from 2 to 3 p.m. Central on Teams to connect directly with Vendors, Merchants and Operations to resolve issues quickly. If you can’t attend, sessions are held every other Tuesday

events_with_summaries.title: Dept. 13: Tide evo Launch
events_with_summaries.status: Review for Publish review - No Comms
events_with_summaries.summary_text: Dept. 13: Tide evo is a category-defining innovation from the #1 laundry brand, reinventing detergent as a lightweight, waterless sheet with no fillers, no plastic bottles, and sustainable packaging. Backed by 3 billion or more (3B+) national impressions and strong Walmart.com visibility, in-store displays begin March 7 with a full rollout by April 11.

events_with_summaries.title: Dept. 40: Modularizing of the Health & Wellness Wall
events_with_summaries.status: Review for Publish review - No Comms
events_with_summaries.summary_text:
Dept. 40: Modularizing of the Health & Wellness Wall

The Health & Wellness Wall is being modularized to create a cohesive, better-merchandised space that improves tracking, maximizes monthly space use, increases sell-through, reduces Nil Picks, and strengthens OPD performance on top categories. Feature Space & Sales (FSS) will be replaced with C110 Modular on the Quarterly Relay starting in March; features will run only in space designated as Dept. 40/Health and Wellness, with store choice available in remaining sections.

events_with_summaries.title: Oversized Picking Safety Update
events_with_summaries.status: Review for Publish review - No Comms
events_with_summaries.summary_text:
Store Fulfillment: Oversized Picking Safety Update

When a customer orders a high quantity of a single heavy item like water, mulch or soil, GIF will automatically split the pick walk to reduce strain and promote safer cart handling. If prompted, start the new walk with a new L-cart and follow all on-screen directions.

events_with_summaries.title: Dept 72: Soundbar Out-of-Stock Solutions
events_with_summaries.status: Review for Publish review - No Comms
events_with_summaries.summary_text: Dept. 72: Three Samsung soundbars are being discontinued with no restock, so starting Feb. 21 stores will receive two JBL replacement models to keep the modular in-stock. Use the lookup tool to confirm which JBL item(s) you’ll get, place them in the former Samsung modular space (double-face as needed), and note pricing is already updated in the system.

events_with_summaries.title: Dept. 72: PC New Software Display, Storage Update
events_with_summaries.status: Review for Publish review - No Comms
events_with_summaries.summary_text:
Dept. 72: PC Software Display Setup

Week 50 shipment: magnetic frame + scannable insert (set immediately; ignore “Hold for Anderson”).

Place on Electronics cash wrap near POS; message must face customer.

Insert scannable sheet for checkout purchase.

Upcoming POS prompt will remind associates to suggest PC software with laptop purchases.

Featured SKUs:

Norton (VPN & 360 tiers), McAfee Total Protection, HR Block 2025 (Basic & Deluxe).

Actions:

Install new placemat at cash wrap.

Ensure price tags are printed/placed on laptop PC software fixtures.

Questions: submit store ticket or email Tyler Jackson.

Dept. 72: Digital Storage Update

Industry-wide inventory constraints on SSDs & PC memory.

Working to improve in-stocks for Week 16 modulars.

Impacted items (no current restock date) include select:

Kingston FURY DDR5 memory

NVIDIA RTX 5070 Ti

Samsung T7/T9 portable SSDs

Samsung 990 EVO/PRO SSDs

Seagate One Touch SSD

Supply improvements ongoing; may pivot to alternative SKUs as needed.

Dept. 72: PC New Software Display, Storage Update

Summarized: Dept. 72: Set up the PC Software scan-to-buy display at Electronics checkout now. Digital storage is supply-constrained, and some Solid-State Drive/Personal Computer (SSD/PC) memory SKUs don’t have restock dates yet.

events_with_summaries.title: Dept. 90:  Fairlife Supply Update
events_with_summaries.status: Review for Publish review - No Comms
events_with_summaries.summary_text:
Dept. 90: Fairlife Supply Update

Starting Week 5 (Feb. 28–March 6), expect increased Fairlife shipments and better in-stocks in Dept. 90. A new Fairlife production facility is now shipping, boosting capacity to meet demand.

Why it matters: Fairlife is one of the category’s fastest growers—up 25% in two years, with another 15% expected this year. Customers want the lower sugar, higher protein, lactose-free benefits.

events_with_summaries.title: Dept. 95: Gatorade Is Investing in a Lower EDLP
events_with_summaries.status: Review for Publish review - No Comms
events_with_summaries.summary_text:
Dept. 95: Gatorade 20-oz 8-packs — lower EDLP

Gatorade is investing in 20-oz 8-packs, dropping EDLP to $6.98 (from $7.98) starting Monday, Feb. 23. The lower price is designed to reengage customers and drive higher demand for the pack size and the category.

To build awareness, an FSS endcap is planned (Plan IDs 230284 and 230288). Depending on the store, the feature runs Feb. 21–March 6 or Feb. 21–April 3. Feature stores will receive an initial PO distribution of 20–50 cases across five flavors.

Take action: If you’re an FSS feature store, partner with your Pepsi rep to execute the endcap to plan. If not, identify a strong display location to highlight the new value. Direct questions to your Pepsi rep or the Home Office team.

events_with_summaries.title: Dept. 95: Introducing Gatorade Lower Sugar and No Artificials
events_with_summaries.status: Review for Publish review - No Comms
events_with_summaries.summary_text:
Dept. 95: Introducing Gatorade Lower Sugar and No Artificials

Gatorade is launching a Lower Sugar/No Artificials line to address top sports drink concerns: sugar and artificial colors/flavors. Shoppers get the same taste and electrolytes with 75% less sugar and no artificial flavors or colors. Launch is Monday, Feb. 23, supported by major media.

Week 5 (Feb. 28–March 6): stores in the FSS plan should build a four-flavor, 28-oz endcap (Plan ID 227476) and will receive DSD PO support. If your store opted in, place display-ready pallets on the sales floor during the Week 4, 7 and 11 waves, and complete the 28-oz modular swap to the new items. Direct questions to your Pepsi rep or the Home Office merchant team.

events_with_summaries.title: Dept. 95: Keep On The Border Chips In-stock
events_with_summaries.status: Review for Publish review - No Comms
events_with_summaries.summary_text:
Dept. 95: Keep On The Border Chips In-stock

On The Border Tortilla Chips (item 598056711) are on Rollback through April and selling fast, but nil picks are rising because product is missing from the sidecounter. While it’s set in the DSD tortilla chip section, it ships through the warehouse and can be missed during stocking.

Take action: Have associates check the tortilla chip sidecounter daily and keep it full. If you need more product, order through the Store Feature Order Tool (SFOT).

events_with_summaries.title: Dept. 95: Poppi Beverages Will Transition to Pepsi DSD
events_with_summaries.status: Review for Publish review - No Comms
events_with_summaries.summary_text:
Dept. 95: Poppi to Pepsi DSD (effective Monday, March 2)

Starting Monday, March 2, Poppi beverages will transition to Pepsi direct store delivery (DSD). Pepsi will own delivery, ordering, stocking, rotation, merchandising and shelf/display maintenance for all Poppi items, including modular sets, secondary placements and promos.

Why it matters: Expect improved in-stocks, faster replenishment, less backroom handling and more consistent on-shelf availability—reducing manual ordering and outs.

Take action: Confirm on-hands are accurate and modulars are set correctly. Keep filling shelves and displays with current inventory. Connect with your Pepsi field rep before March 2 to prevent disruptions.

events_with_summaries.title: Dept. 95: Updated Energy Sidekicks
events_with_summaries.status: Review for Publish review - No Comms
events_with_summaries.summary_text:
Dept. 95: Energy Sidekicks Are Being Updated

Beginning Week 3, permanent Energy sidekicks are being updated to increase holding power and add seasonal/LTO space. Monster’s permanent sidekick will transition to Alani+Celsius, using existing Monster fixtures; Monster will not have a permanent sidekick in stores through Week 8.

In Week 9, Monster’s permanent sidekick returns to select stores as an add-on — not a replacement — alongside existing Red Bull and Alani+Celsius sidekicks. Suppliers are aligned and expected to complete modulars to the new standard.

events_with_summaries.title: D80 Prima Della Update
events_with_summaries.status: Review for Publish review - No Comms
events_with_summaries.summary_text:
Prima Della Pre-Sliced Honey Turkey Shortage

Temporary shortage: Pre-sliced Prima Della Honey Roasted Turkey Breast (items 578738649, 671139503) is on supplier constraint, so replenishment is temporarily turned off. Bulk Prima Della Honey Roasted Turkey is not impacted, and suppliers will continue shipping pre-sliced product through Week 4. Replenishment is expected to resume between Weeks 8 and 13.

Take action: Keep the Deli case full by expanding facings of adjacent pre-sliced turkey items and using bulk product where appropriate. After recovery, traited stores will receive two cases of Honey Roasted Turkey to help rebuild in-stocks.

events_with_summaries.title: Dept. 93: Fish Today, Corned Beef Tomorrow
events_with_summaries.status: Review for Publish review - No Comms
events_with_summaries.summary_text:
Dept. 93: Lent focus items

Lent is here. Set Dept. 93 up for success by prioritizing 41/60 shrimp, salmon and tilapia. Ensure features are set, aisle locations are signed, and seafood modulars are worked daily to protect in-stocks and capture the seasonal lift.

Dept. 93: Item in the spotlight: Great Value tilapia (Rollback)

Great Value Frozen Skinless Tilapia Fillets, 4-pound, is on Rollback for $17.47. With Lent demand, plan for about a 20% lift (roughly 30 units per week) — but only if it’s on the floor, full and easy to shop. Stock up and feature it prominently.

Dept. 93:  St. Patrick’s Day: corned beef

St. Patrick’s Day is March 17, and corned beef shipments are building now. Feature corned beef and cabbage to cue the holiday. Do not mark down early; shipments will be monitored and adjusted to balance sell-through. Watch for the fast-growing Corned Beef Kit and use the lookup tool to confirm if your store will receive it.

Dept. 93: Pork Rollbacks: flag the value

As grilling season ramps up, pork Rollbacks are a key sales driver — if customers see the savings. Make sure every Rollback item is clearly flagged in Dept. 93 to convert traffic into units and reinforce value across the assortment.

events_with_summaries.title: Dept. 94: Produce Updates
events_with_summaries.status: Review for Publish review - No Comms
events_with_summaries.summary_text:
1) Produce in-stock risks: Easter basket focus

Keep shelves full for Easter basket produce. Bulk plums are pressured due to early Chile season and nonaccepted varieties (get well Week 4). Green onions and cilantro are weather-impacted; cilantro improves around Week 5, green onions around Week 8. Bulk corn is tight after major Florida freeze losses; protect packaged corn. Carrots and organic lettuces/celery are also weather-impacted. Watch March-April risk on watermelons, blueberries and cabbage.

2) Corn supply tightening: substitute and protect quality

Corn supply will tighten through early April after historic freezes in Florida, driving intermittent outs and higher nil picks. Expect cosmetic issues like grey or dry husks; confirm the ear is bad before discarding. When bulk is short, substitute with packaged corn when possible. Vendors are prioritizing Walmart; current get-well is about Week 10. Do not accept secondary-market corn due to poor quality, and verify retails weekly as conditions change.

3) Cabbage: cosmetic damage, keep accepting

Cold weather caused cosmetic issues on cabbage, but in-stocks should remain above goal and no major quality problems are expected. Continue to accept cabbage that arrives without outer leaves; it does not increase shrink or reduce eating quality. Ensure your store has taken the appropriate retail actions on green cabbage. A backup supply is being secured, with additional shipments sent as availability allows. Get-well is expected by month-end.

4) Carrots: supply risk, protect Easter basket items

Weather in California and the Southeast is pressuring carrot supply, but sourcing is secured across regions and sizing exceptions are in place to keep product flowing. Expect in-stock risk for about three weeks; total category in-stocks are running about 92%-97%. Priority is on baby peeled carrots for Easter baskets, including a Rollback on 2-pound baby peeled carrots at $1.82 from Feb. 23-April 23.

events_with_summaries.title: Week 04 - Dept. 81: Easter Prep and Brown 'N Serve
events_with_summaries.status: Review for Publish review - No Comms
events_with_summaries.summary_text:
Dept. 81: Easter Prep and Brown 'N Serve

Prep now for Easter staples in Dept. 81, including Brown ’N Serve rolls (store-managed). Check the backroom regularly for arrivals and keep the modular home location full. As the holiday nears, plan cross-merchandising by setting 4-way sides or saddlebag displays near the ham section to drive add-on sales.

events_with_summaries.title: Week 04 - Dept. 98: Spring Features and High Margin Items
events_with_summaries.status: Review for Publish review - No Comms
events_with_summaries.summary_text:
Dept. 98: Spring Feature Planning and Post-Seasonal Cleanup

Spring is a chance to grow baskets with solution-driven, high-margin displays. Spotlight angel food items: the 14-ounce cake ring (item 563001974) for mass displays, the 10-ounce bar (item 662658723) for smaller occasions, and 6-count dessert shells (item 653323529) as a key add-on.

Create a seasonal destination — especially a “Strawberry Shortcake” set — by cross-merchandising Bakery with fresh strawberries (Produce) and Cool Whip (Dairy/Frozen) across frozen, chilled and ambient areas. As the season winds down, stay ahead of end-of-season cleanup: keep the sales floor organized, manage CVPs daily, work markdowns, and clear any lingering Mardi Gras inventory.

events_with_summaries.title: Clean Floor Safety
events_with_summaries.status: Review for Publish review - No Comms
events_with_summaries.summary_text:
Clean Floor Safety

Clean floors prevent slips, trips, and falls. Clean spills immediately, or guard the area and get help. Never leave spills unattended. Keep aisles clear, remove debris, and report wet spots, damaged flooring, or repeat hazards right away.

events_with_summaries.status: Review for Publish review - No Comms
